items.evidence_span: different approaches are adopted to establish long-context capabilities for large language models [4, 14, 5, 8, 9, 16, 2]
we use GPT-4 [13] to synthesize $3.5 \mathrm{~K}$ long-context training data
we mix $5 \mathrm{~K}$ instances randomly chosen from RedPajama [6]
We also mix LongAlpaca [5] in the training set
We use QLoRA [7] to efficiently fine-tune the model.
No parallel strategy is required thanks to the efficient implementation from Unsloth [1].
Thirdly, we evaluate our model on LongBench [3], which contains a variety of real-world long-context tasks.
Forthly, we employ the English Long-Book QA and the Long-Book Summarization task from InfiniteBench [17] to assess the model's performance on really long context.
Lastly, in Table 3, we compare the zero-shot performance of our model and the baselines on MMLU [10] benchmark.
